search_queries_used: site:entnet.org evaluation neck mass adults guideline history targeted exam follow-up 2017
site:nice.org.uk NG12 neck lump head neck cancer
candidate_sources_rejected: adult neck-mass guideline applied to children or known thyroid/salivary masses
automatic cancer diagnosis, antibiotic, CT, MRI, FNA, endoscopy, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: The AAO-HNSF scope explicitly excludes children and known thyroid, salivary, mandibular, and dental origins.
No diagnosis, investigation, medicine, procedure, referral, or urgency is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults older than 18 with an undifferentiated neck mass; children and known thyroid, salivary, dental, or mandibular origin require separate evidence.
setting_applicability: Primary, dental, urgent, emergency, or ENT initial neck-mass documentation.
UAE_applicability: International guidance requires UAE head-and-neck oncology, imaging, pathology, FNA, ENT, dental, and referral adaptation.
recency_verification: The official AAO-HNSF 2017 neck-mass and current NICE NG12 exact sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The 2017 AAO-HNSF guideline remains listed as current; NICE NG12 was last updated in 2026.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact adult neck-mass risk-history section supports onset, duration, progression, symptom impact, associated upper-aerodigestive features, relevant negatives, and clinician concern without asserting malignancy.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting duration, fluctuation, infectious context, persistence, associated upper-aerodigestive features, and clinician concern in adults without asserting malignancy.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact targeted-examination section supports clinician-performed ENT, oral, throat, and neck examination without auto-populating a mass or mucosal finding.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-performed examination of the oral cavity, oropharynx, laryngeal region, neck, and related mucosal sites when within competence; no finding is auto-populated.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact follow-up and education section supports concerns, reviewed specialist reports, clinician-entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions without ordering imaging, FNA, endoscopy, or referral.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording patient education, questions, planned follow-up, clinician concern, and already completed imaging or pathology context. Antibiotics, imaging, FNA, endoscopy, and referral decisions are not mapped.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact NICE neck-lump and thyroid-lump recommendations were reviewed for age- and persistence-qualified cancer-recognition context; no automatic cancer or referral text was mapped.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician recognition of recommendation-qualified persistent unexplained hoarseness, unexplained or persistent neck lump, oral ulceration lasting more than 3 weeks, lip or oral-cavity lump, red or red-and-white oral patch, and unexplained thyroid lump. It does not assert cancer or generate referral.

unresolved_source_gaps: Age, duration, site, size, mobility, tenderness, infection, smoking, alcohol, HPV, dental and thyroid context, examination, imaging, pathology, diagnosis, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
